Feature / Integration: Task execution plan creation (single vs discussion, critic, synthesis)
What is actually wired: Creates participantRoleIds, primaryRoleId, criticRoleId, orchestrationSummary, and adaptive-orchestrator flags before task execution.
Gaps / risks: Still depends on upstream orchestration inputs and role metadata quality.
Verdict: Good implementation of core task planning behavior.

What is actually wired: For non-quick, untagged prompts, discussion mode and adaptive orchestrator are preferred automatically.
Gaps / risks: Still seeded by heuristic plan generation before orchestrator refinement.

Feature / Integration: Adaptive orchestrator with full enabled-agent context
What is actually wired: Enabled agent list plus per-agent summary/strengths/limits/useWhen are passed into the orchestrator prompt before brief runs.
Gaps / risks: JSON-plan parsing is still a hard contract and can fail closed to fallback heuristics.
Verdict: This is a real implementation, not just prompt wording.

What is actually wired: Prompt intent and primary-role seeds still use regex/keyword heuristics before orchestration.
Gaps / risks: This is the main place where the system is still rule-heavy instead of purely orchestrator-first.
Verdict: Partially aligned; not yet pure orchestrator-first.

What is actually wired: Agent exists in TS presets and Tauri task prompt packs/resources with alias/refactor role behavior.
Gaps / risks: Effectiveness depends on prompt quality; not a separate engine.
Verdict: Successfully added as a real task agent.

Feature / Integration: Task role execution can use web-backed models
What is actually wired: Task role execution routes to web_provider_run when the selected thread model is web-backed instead of always starting a Codex thread.
Gaps / risks: Tasks still do not expose WEB/STEEL and WEB/LIGHTPANDA in the task model dropdown.
Verdict: Real behavior change; properly wired for AI GPT/Gemini/Grok/Perplexity/Claude.

Feature / Integration: Collaboration stages preserve selected web-backed runtime
What is actually wired: Brief/orchestrate/critique/final stages can keep using the selected web-backed thread model rather than forcing Codex stage models.
Gaps / risks: Still separate from direct external CDP providers in tasks model menu.

Open source / external source: Internal web_worker automation over provider websites
What is actually wired: Standard web providers run through web worker bridge commands (provider/run, openSession, resetSession, cancel).
Gaps / risks: Relies on bridge/browser automation stability and website behavior.
Verdict: This part is genuinely present and is the current web-AI path.

What is actually wired: Dedicated bridge server, auto-install/start/health/fetch commands, role knowledge integration, and dashboard crawl integration.
Test evidence: Behavior is covered indirectly through role knowledge pipeline tests.
Gaps / risks: Rust-side provider branch lacks focused unit tests.
Verdict: One of the most complete external integrations in the repo.

Implementation depth: Production path for role knowledge/document-style fetch
What is actually wired: Python fetch script exists, provider health/install/fetch path exists, and role knowledge prefers/falls back across crawl4ai and scrapling.
Gaps / risks: Not used as the universal fetch layer; concentrated in role knowledge/provider routing.
Verdict: Properly integrated for the intended document-extraction slice.

What is actually wired: Health and fetch path exist via CDP, and engine routes external provider runs to the external path when provider is steel.
Gaps / risks: Requires environment configuration; no strong dedicated Rust tests found; not exposed in tasks runtime model menu.
Verdict: More than a stub, but not fully surfaced everywhere.

What is actually wired: Health/fetch path exists as external provider and workflow domain recognizes the executor label.
Gaps / risks: Experimental only; not surfaced in tasks model menu; limited test evidence.
Verdict: Scaffold plus runnable branch, but still clearly experimental.

What is actually wired: Provider appears in capability registry and runtime preference ordering, but health/fetch explicitly report not wired yet.
Gaps / risks: Not actually executable yet despite being referenced in ordering metadata.

What is actually wired: Dynamic research jobs can choose scrapy_playwright strategy and execute a helper batch collector through via runtime server_impl.
Gaps / risks: Not wired as a general crawl-provider fetch API; concentrated in research_storage pipeline.
Verdict: Real integration, but scope-limited to research batch collection.

What is actually wired: Researcher role can plan/execute collection jobs and inject resulting context into the role prompt before answer generation.
Gaps / risks: Quality depends on collection planning and provider availability.
Verdict: A meaningful business-logic integration, not just UI.

Feature / Integration: WEB / STEEL and WEB / LIGHTPANDA surfaced to users
What is actually wired: Workflow domain knows executors, but tasks model dropdown only exposes Codex + AI GPT/Gemini/Grok/Perplexity/Claude options.
Gaps / risks: User cannot directly choose these from tasks composer even though runtime code exists.
Verdict: Important product gap between backend capability and surfaced UX.
